questions.question: Will the average global temperature in 2024 exceed 2023?

flash_rationales.reasoning: Every month since June 2023 - 13 months in a row - has ranked as the planets hottest since records began  compared with the corresponding month in previous years  the European Unions Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) said in a monthly bulletin. Reuters July 8th   Sea surface temperatures were record warm for the 15th consecutive month. NOAA Climate.gov July 16th  2024   Im at 96.5  not 100  only because the shift to LaNiña with the current temperatures and pressures  and over such a short transition time is unprecedented so the outcome is not known with certainty. Forecast for the next few months are for continuing record heat  so it would need to experience a very rapid cooling to offset the anomalous months that we have already experienced.
flash_rationales.question: Will the average global temperature in 2024 exceed 2023?

In fact this temperature anomaly is a bit higher than the one from the first six months of 2024. So temperatures need to still have a fairly high anomaly the rest of the year. This is very likely (90%).

questions.question: Will Elon Musk be the richest person in the world on December 31, 2025?

flash_rationales.reasoning: Musk $249.9  Bezos 205.2B  Bernard Arnault 190.4B  Larry Ellison 173.3  Larry Page 151.8  Gates 135.2. Jen-Hsun (Nvidia). If Tesla drops from its current 250 to 150  Musk could easily drop a spot. Jen Hsun has seen a meteoric rise  that has stayed been volatile but somewhat plateaued for a month. Considering it has gone up 157% in the last year  a similar jump could see him topple Musk. Tesla has pressure from Chinese EVs. Metaculus is at 49% I will go a little higher at 60%. Musk is playing a shrewd election calculation. He is heavily and publicly supporting Trump. If Trump wins  he will have his ear (oops  bad pun). If Trump loses  Biden is pro EV anyway. He may expand the base of people willing to buy Teslas if the Trump supporters start to see it as desirable. His cybertruck has been a laughing stock among serious off road and rural people  maybe as much based on the radical style change as lack of practical bed use of a typical work truck. This is still a tough crowd to win. He also risks a backlash being too closely associated with Trump. Musks Teslas could go the way of the Hummer if they fall out of fashion on ideological/identity purchases.
flash_rationales.question: Will Elon Musk be the richest person in the world on December 31, 2025?

flash_rationales.reasoning: Tesla annual/quarterly operating income history and growth rate from 2010 to 2024. Operating income can be defined as income after operating expenses have been deducted and before interest payments and taxes have been deducted.   Tesla operating income for the quarter ending March 31  2024 was $1.171B  a 56.04% decline year-over-year.   Tesla operating income for the twelve months ending March 31  2024 was $7.398B  a 41.83% decline year-over-year.   Tesla annual operating income for 2023 was $8.891B  a 34.89% decline from 2022.   Tesla annual operating income for 2022 was $13.656B  a 109.35% increase from 2021.   Tesla annual operating income for 2021 was $6.523B  a 227.13% increase from 2020.   Going low as I think Tesla is heading for a fall but lots of others have predicted this and been left with egg on their faces!

questions.question: Will there be more than 25 named storms during Atlantic Hurricane Season?

flash_rationales.reasoning: With 3 tropical storms so far and an average of 13.75 storms in the period between halfway through July and the end of the season  the base rate forecast using a Poisson distribution is roughly 1.4%.   Empirically this happened twice (2020 and 2005) in the past 20 seasons (10%) implying that the Poisson distribution is not the most accurate model. Hurricane seasons seem to have sort of a fat tail.   Meteorological agencies predict a very active hurricane season with a high number of named storms (22 according to UKMO  21 according to NOAA  26 according to TSR  25 according to CSU  23 according to UA). Averaging these forecasts and using the NOAA 70% confidence interval of 17-25 outputs a 27% prediction using a Poisson distribution  and about 30% using a normal distribution.
flash_rationales.question: Will there be more than 25 named storms during Atlantic Hurricane Season?

flash_evaluation.summary: The rationale is excellent, combining historical base rates, meteorological agency forecasts, and statistical modeling (Poisson and Normal distributions) to derive a calibrated probability.
main_strengths: quantitative statistical modeling
comprehensive use of expert agency forecasts
acknowledgment of fat-tail distribution in historical data
main_weaknesses: limited discussion of specific upcoming climate factors like La Niña
flash_evaluation.raw_model_response:

flash_rationales.reasoning: We are moving quickly from an El Niño to La Niña in the Pacific basin. NOAA is forecasting a range of 17 to 25 total named storms   Climatologically  we are transitioning from the homegrown storms of June and early July to the more long-track storms that roll off Africa and take days to cross the Atlantic. Saharan dust also reaches its peak concentration  helping to gobble up prospective storms.   This year  there’s also a third thing  the Madden-Julian Oscillation or MJO  a phase that promotes Pacific development and fills the Atlantic side with sinking air to further deter storm development.   The MJO is a cluster of clouds and rain that moves across the globe every 30-60 days. It can enhance or suppress tropical activity  depending upon what part of the world its currently in.   Flipping the switch   The opposite will be true heading into August. We are  of course  heading into the climatological peak of hurricane season  which will naturally promote storm development. In addition  the MJO will move back into a more favorable position for tropical development.   Im starting at 8. The NOAA forecasts are for a range of 17-25 and we had an early start to the season  but the lull  MJO and time between El Niño and La Niña raise high uncertainty. I am not comfortable to go up to the 46% forecast of Polymarket. It is far of of the NOAA estimate and as others have mentioned  there are only two previous years that have exceeded 25. [redacted to maintain anonymity] Part of this includes that localised changes that each system brings in terms of cooling or warming different regions and bringing more or less rain or pressure  to different areas within even countries the size of Mexico. These are not simple systems  especially when adding the general shift towards warming ocean temperatures. My guess is that it will be a severe and long season  but my uncertainty leads to fat tails. With global warming my default is to forecast higher than most estimates as it often turns out correct  but 21-25 captures the high end of the estimate  and the El Niño La Niña rapid shift may not moderate extreme temperatures. We are in the unknown without a clear base rate for this short of a transition combined with the already increased heat of the oceans.
flash_rationales.question: Will there be more than 25 named storms during Atlantic Hurricane Season?

questions.question: Will the majority of new cars sold be electric vehicles by the end of 2030?

flash_rationales.reasoning: The definition of cars and electric vehicles has not been made explicit.   The definition provided by the DoE is as follows:   An EV is defined as a vehicle that can be powered by an electric motor that draws electricity from a battery and is capable of being charged from an external source. An EV includes both a vehicle that can only be powered by an electric motor that draws electricity from a battery (all-electric vehicle) and a vehicle that can be powered by an electric motor that draws electricity from a battery and by an internal combustion engine (plug-in hybrid electric vehicle).   It therefore includes EVs and PHEVs.   This said this forecast depends on two main things: a) where are new cars being sold  and b) which types of cars are being sold.   Some of the largest markets (China  EU) are adopting more and more electric vehicles. Other large markets (US  India) are lagging.   OWID charts show that the current market share for EVs and PHEVs is about 18%. It has grown rapidly in the past 5 years  but it is not exploding exponentially.   I believe that one of the largest limiting factors here is on the supply side. Car manufacturers work with platforms that have an expected lifetime on the market of about 10-15 years. Given the fact that IC platforms most often cannot be easily used to manufacture EVs and PHEVs  as long as they are not substituted for new platforms  the share of electric vehicles on the market cannot reach very high levels.
flash_rationales.question: Will the majority of new cars sold be electric vehicles by the end of 2030?

flash_evaluation.summary: The rationale provides a strong, well-reasoned argument that addresses the resolution criteria, uses current market data, and identifies a specific structural constraint (manufacturing platform lifecycles) as a limiting factor.
main_strengths: identifies specific supply-side constraints
references current global market share (18%)
clarifies the inclusion of PHEVs
main_weaknesses: lacks a detailed quantitative projection of the necessary growth rate to reach 50%

flash_rationales.reasoning: As far as I can tell  there is no explicit clarification on the manifold markets site whether or not Plug-in Hybrids count as electric vehicles for the purpose of this question. Im assuming they do not count and only purely electric cars with no combustion engines do.   Recent numbers from China have been very encouraging and given electromobility bulls new hope. China will almost certainly reach that threshhold way before 2030  but most other important markets  notably China and Europe (with big differences within countries) lag behind. In both  I see charging infrastructure as the major chokepoint - these countries have been slow to add large amounts of charging stations and could hardly even deal with that many electric vehicles being added to the fleet quickly. We also see protectionist measures aganst cheap Chinese EV imports  futher hampering sales growth in Europe.   Could it still happen? Yes. There have been substantial price drop in batteries  making electric cars more competitive. Ultimately  the trendline points to the 50% threshhold being reached  but my estimate is that it will be reached only by 2032 or 2033.

flash_rationales.reasoning:
0. Yes to China no for the rest of the world.
1. Many to most older homes (in the US) cannot handle the amps needed to install a Level 2 charger without upgrading the wiring this is a significant cost overlooked by pundits; Compare/contrast the US with China---the skew of Chinese housing towards modern multi-family buildings makes converting to a charging infrastructure much more easier and cheaper. 2. Theoretically electric powertrains are a magnitude better than gasoline-engines in terms of maintenance and reliability---however in the real world the reliability of electric cars is (anecdotally) all over-the-map. The dominant failure point of electric vehicles is the motor and infotainment system which wholly depends on the quality of parts used and the competence of the assembly. 3. Expected resale value of electric vehicles in the US. One aspect that buoys the resale value of gasoline values in the US is that even a poorly maintained vehicle can be kept going by a 3rd or 4th owner with $500-worth of repairs on a semi-annual basis using aftermarket parts. With electric vehicles it is still uncertain whether for example the 4th owner (who makes $40 000 per year) of a Tesla 3 with 150 000 miles will be able to afford to use that car---anecdotes are all over the map with the most prominent example being a Tesla S owner who has driven >1 200 000 miles in his Tesla on the 4th battery pack and has gone through 14 motors. Severe depreciation of 10 year old EVs will have a cascade effect which will eventually affect original owners. 4. If there is a sustained and pronounced heat wave in the Northeast that pushes demand to the limits of the regional electric grid do not be surprised if an emergency measure is to forcibly throttle the electricity consumption of electric charging networks. Add the possibility of throttle/brownout anxiety to range anxiety. 5. Electric cars perform poorly during the extremes of northern US continental winters. (compare/contrast winter temperature range in the US Rust Belt versus Los Angeles London Shanghai).
flash_rationales.question: Will the majority of new cars sold be electric vehicles by the end of 2030?

The best team would be Puerto Rico who have odds at +30 000 (that means -- if you bet $100 on Puerto Rico to win -- and they do -- youll win $30 000).   So  USA likely finishes as the best team in the group stage  which means  based on how theyre doing the draw  the USA will be one of the top two teams in the whole thing.   https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basketball_at_the_2024_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_tournament#Knockout_stage   That means  theyre unlikely to face one of the weaker teams that remain in the tournament in the quarterfinals. My guess is they wont be close to challenged until the semi-finals.   Giving them a really high percentage here.
flash_rationales.question: Will the U.S. win the gold medal in the Men's Basketball at the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris?
